seoTitle: How to Type Chinese in Google Docs and Slides
metaDescription: Learn how to type Simplified or Traditional Chinese in Google Docs and Slides using the built-in Input Tools and Pinyin keyboard, no extra software needed.
intro: Typing Chinese characters in Google Docs or Slides doesn't require installing separate keyboard software or fighting with broken fonts. Google Workspace has a native Input Tools system that lets you activate a Pinyin keyboard or handwriting pad directly inside your document. This guide covers turning on the language, typing with Pinyin, and adjusting fonts for presentations.

steps.heading: Add Chinese to your language settings
steps.body: Open File > Language in your Google Doc or Slide, then scroll through the language list and select your preferred Chinese variant.

steps.heading: Activate the Input Tools keyboard
steps.body: Once selected, an Input Tools keyboard icon appears in your toolbar. Click the dropdown and turn on the Chinese Pinyin input method.

steps.heading: Type using Pinyin
steps.body: Type standard romanized letters to trigger a predictive character selection grid, then press the matching number key or spacebar to commit your chosen word.

steps.heading: Try handwriting input
steps.body: Toggle the handwriting pencil icon to draw characters directly with your mouse or stylus instead of typing Pinyin.

steps.heading: Adjust fonts for slides
steps.body: Highlight your Chinese text and switch to a Chinese-optimized typeface like Noto Sans or KaiTi to keep characters crisp and readable on presentation slides.

faq.question: Do I need to install anything to type Chinese in Google Docs?
faq.answer: No, the Input Tools feature is built into Google Workspace and works entirely in the browser.

faq.question: Can I switch between Simplified and Traditional Chinese?
faq.answer: Yes, both character standards are available as separate language options in the Input Tools selector.

faq.question: Will Chinese text display correctly for collaborators without the input method enabled?
faq.answer: Yes, once typed, the text is stored as standard Unicode characters and displays correctly for anyone viewing the document.

faq.question: Does this work on Google Slides as well as Docs?
faq.answer: Yes, the same Input Tools system is available across both Google Docs and Google Slides.
